mapping.a_element: non-commutative space and C*-algebra constructed from a tiling
mapping.b_element: groupoids obtained from projection point patterns

mapping.a_element: topology on the groupoid induced by translations of the tiling
mapping.b_element: pattern groupoid derived from projection method tilings

mapping.a_element: scaled ordered K_0-group furnishing gap labelling
mapping.b_element: cohomology of the point pattern as cocycle cohomology of the pattern groupoid

mapping.a_element: invariant measure on the hull of the tiling
mapping.b_element: topological invariants for projection method patterns

brief_justification: Both papers construct algebraic/topological structures (C*-algebras and groupoids) from tilings or point patterns to derive invariants (K-theory/gap labelling in A, cohomology in B). The machinery involves analyzing groupoids associated with tilings and using their properties to compute invariants, indicating a shared underlying method in non-commutative geometry and topological dynamics.
